title: Word Break
description:
Given a string `s` and a dictionary of strings `wordDict`, return `true` if `s` can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of one or more dictionary words.

Note that the same word in the dictionary may be reused multiple times in the segmentation.

**Example 1:**
```
Input: s = "leetcode", wordDict = ["leet","code"]
Output: true
Explanation: Return true because "leetcode" can be segmented as "leet code".
```

**Example 2:**
```
Input: s = "applepenapple", wordDict = ["apple","pen"]
Output: true
Explanation: Return true because "applepenapple" can be segmented as "apple pen apple". Note that you are allowed to reuse a dictionary word.
```

**Example 3:**
```
Input: s = "catsandog", wordDict = ["cats","dog","sand","and","cat"]
Output: false
Explanation: No valid segmentation exists for "catsandog".
```

examples.explanation: "leetcode" can be split as "leet" + "code", both of which are in the dictionary.
"applepenapple" can be split as "apple" + "pen" + "apple". Dictionary words can be reused.

constraints: s and wordDict[i] consist of only lowercase English letters
All the strings of wordDict are unique
solution_approach: Use bottom-up dynamic programming. Define dp[i] as whether the substring s[0..i-1] can be segmented using dictionary words. Initialize dp[0] = true (empty string is trivially segmentable). For each position i from 1 to n, check every position j from 0 to i-1: if dp[j] is true and s[j..i-1] exists in the dictionary (use a HashSet for O(1) lookup), then dp[i] = true. The final answer is dp[n]. To optimize, instead of checking all j values, you can iterate over words in the dictionary and check if s ends with that word at position i. The worst-case time complexity is O(n^2 * m) where n is the string length and m is the average word length for substring comparison, though using a hash set reduces the inner check. Space is O(n) for the dp array plus O(total characters in wordDict) for the set.
hints: Think about this problem in terms of subproblems: if you know that s[0..j-1] can be segmented, and s[j..i-1] is a word in the dictionary, what can you say about s[0..i-1]?
Define dp[i] = true if s[0..i-1] can be broken into dictionary words. For each i, check all possible last words ending at position i. Use a set for O(1) word lookups.
Build dp bottom-up from dp[0] = true. For each i from 1 to n, for each j from 0 to i-1, if dp[j] is true and s[j:i] is in the word set, set dp[i] = true and break. Return dp[n].
follow_up_questions: How would you modify this to return all possible segmentations (like Word Break II)?
Could you use a Trie instead of a HashSet for the dictionary to improve performance when the dictionary is very large?
